reasoning: The most advanced Lyme disease vaccine candidate is VLA15 (Pfizer/Valneva), which completed its primary Phase 3 vaccination series in July 2024. A booster dose trial phase will occur around July 2025, and Pfizer has officially stated they plan to seek FDA approval in 2026. Because the regulatory submission won't even happen until 2026, the probability of having an approved and available vaccine by any of the resolution dates (the latest being March 16, 2026) is virtually zero. Additionally, while a Lyme disease vaccine previously existed (LYMErix) but was discontinued in 2002, the Wikipedia article currently leaves the vaccine column blank (resolving as "No"). This implies historical/discontinued vaccines are not counted. The only plausible paths to a "Yes" resolution are a surprise early regulatory submission in another market or a Wikipedia editor prematurely adding a candidate/discontinued vaccine to the list that is mistakenly parsed as a "Yes". This error risk increases slightly with the forecast horizon.
